name: Mammatus Cloud

visual_attributes.morphology: cellular pattern of pouches hanging underneath the base of a cloud
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: individual lobes average diameters of 1–3 kilometers and lengths of 0.5 kilometers
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: grouping of lobes that can vary from isolated clusters to fields spreading over hundreds of kilometers

domain_specific_visual.cloud_type: cumulonimbus, altostratus, cirrus, or volcanic ash clouds
domain_specific_visual.composition: primarily ice, but can include liquid water

functional_attributes.primary_function: indicate severe weather or strong storms

domain_specific_functional.weather_indicator: harbinger of a coming storm or extreme weather system

relationships.description: Mammatus clouds often extend from the base of cumulonimbus clouds.
Mammatus clouds are often indicative of strong storms.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: global, associated with severe weather systems
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: often seen as ominous due to association with severe weather

contrastive_constraints: NOT smooth cloud bases without lobes
NOT unrelated to severe weather systems
